questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'super' keyword in Java?
options: A) To declare a class as a subclass
B) To call methods from a parent class
C) To create a new instance of a class
D) To access member variables of a parent class
questions.explanation: The 'super' keyword is used to call methods from a parent class, allowing for code reuse and flexibility in object-oriented programming.

questions.question: What is a multilevel hierarchy in Java?
options: A) A class that inherits from another class
B) A class that implements an interface
C) A class that has multiple inheritance
D) A class that extends another class
questions.explanation: A multilevel hierarchy in Java refers to a class that extends another class, which itself may also extend another class, creating a chain of inheritance.

questions.question: When should you use a constructor in Java?
options: A) To declare a class as a subclass
B) To create a new instance of a class
C) To call methods from a parent class
D) To initialize objects with default values
questions.explanation: Constructors are used to initialize objects with default values or to provide an alternative way of creating objects without using the default constructor.

questions.question: What is the difference between a superclass and a subclass?
options: A) A superclass is a class that is inherited from, while a subclass is the class that inherits from it
B) A superclass is a class that implements an interface, while a subclass is a class that extends it
C) A superclass is a class that has multiple inheritance, while a subclass is a class that extends it
D) A superclass is a class that is used as a parent class, while a subclass is a class that is used as a child class
questions.explanation: A superclass is a class that is inherited from, while a subclass is the class that inherits from it, inheriting its properties and behavior.

questions.question: Can a class have multiple superclasses?
options: A) Yes, a class can have multiple superclasses
B) No, a class can only have one superclass
C) It depends on the type of inheritance
D) It depends on the compiler
questions.explanation: No, a class can only have one superclass in Java. Multiple inheritance is not supported, and the compiler will prevent attempts to define a class with multiple superclasses.
